sections.title: Overview & Evaluation Criteria
sections.promptHint: Describe what is being evaluated and why. List the evaluation criteria with assigned weights (must sum to 100%). Criteria examples: functionality, security, pricing, integrations, support, vendor stability, ease of implementation.
sections.ragQuery: evaluation purpose criteria requirements decision context

sections.title: Vendor Profiles
sections.promptHint: One profile per vendor: company overview, target customer, pricing model (if known), key features and capabilities, and honest 2–3 strengths and weaknesses relevant to the evaluation criteria.
sections.ragQuery: vendors products features pricing capabilities strengths weaknesses

sections.title: Scoring Matrix
sections.promptHint: A weighted scoring table. Rows = criteria, Columns = vendors. Score each vendor 1–5 per criterion. Multiply by weight. Sum to get weighted total. Highlight the winner. Note any disqualifying factors separately.

sections.title: Risk Assessment
sections.promptHint: For each vendor: assess vendor stability (funding, longevity, market position), lock-in risk (data portability, exit costs), contract terms concerns, and support quality. Use High/Med/Low ratings.
sections.ragQuery: vendor risk stability lock-in contract support reliability

sections.title: Total Cost of Ownership
sections.promptHint: 3-year TCO for each vendor in table format. Include: licensing/subscription, implementation cost, training, ongoing support, and estimated internal engineering time. Sum to total 3-year cost per vendor.
sections.ragQuery: cost pricing licensing implementation support total cost

sections.title: Recommendation
sections.promptHint: State the recommended vendor and the runner-up. The recommendation must follow from the scorecard and TCO. If the top scorer is not recommended, explain why (e.g. a disqualifying risk). Include suggested next steps: proof of concept, contract negotiation, migration planning.
